routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Screen map / 3. Page layouts / 8. Command and read API additions / 11. Accessibility and responsive rules
routes.title: Staff work inbox
routes.dominant_question: Which work item needs action now, and why?
routes.dominant_action: Assign, claim, escalate, or enter the workspace without losing queue context.
routes.artifact_posture: Queue rows summarize current state only; attachments stay subordinate until workspace entry.
step_up_checkpoints: Privileged reassignment or escalation may trigger inline step-up under northbound command law.
routes.stale_view_posture: Rows remain mounted while basis drift rebases inline and mutation affordances fail closed.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Refresh and reconnect preserve filters, row order, and the anchored row.
Notification-open returns to the same queue row when the object remains legal.
No full-page reset for ordinary row updates.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the originating inbox row or the narrowest surviving filter chip.
routes.responsive_fallback: Filter rails compact into chips and the detail drawer redocks beneath the primary stack on narrow screens.
routes.notes: The inbox is the staff collaboration queue root and must update in place.

source_refs.rationale: The route and queue semantics are explicit.
Queue updates, escalation posture, and stale protection are explicitly testable.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Screen map / 3. Page layouts / 8. Command and read API additions / 11. Accessibility and responsive rules
routes.title: Staff work-item workspace
routes.dominant_question: What is the safest next decision on this work item?
routes.dominant_action: Advance the work item while preserving dominant question, settlement posture, and thread continuity.
routes.artifact_posture: Current artifacts, linked context, and history remain visibly distinct inside the same shell.
step_up_checkpoints: Risky status changes and authority-dependent actions stay inline and shell-local.
routes.stale_view_posture: Keep the full calm-shell frame mounted and downgrade unsafe mutations to `NO_SAFE_ACTION` during rebase.
recovery_and_resume_rules: The ordered reading path remains `CONTEXT_BAR -> DECISION_SUMMARY -> ACTION_STRIP -> DETAIL_DRAWER`.
Rebase preserves the same item shell, draft context, and selected module when still legal.
Focus returns to the route-local anchor after module, drawer, or composer transitions.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the originating module tab, composer trigger, or queue row anchor.
routes.responsive_fallback: On narrow screens the drawer collapses below the primary stack without changing module meaning.
routes.notes: This is the canonical calm-shell collaboration workspace and owns same-object/same-shell continuity for work items.

source_refs.rationale: The four-surface order and module grammar are explicit.
The calm-shell ordered reading path is shared law.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Screen map / 3. Page layouts / 8. Command and read API additions / 11. Accessibility and responsive rules
routes.title: Staff customer-visible activity module
routes.dominant_question: What has the customer seen, and what reply is safe now?
routes.dominant_action: Respond or request more information using customer-visible language only.
routes.artifact_posture: Only customer-visible activity and customer-safe attachment posture may appear here.
step_up_checkpoints: Sensitive outbound requests stay bound to the work-item stale-view guard.
routes.stale_view_posture: Preserve the thread and composer while invalidating only the unsafe submission path.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Customer-visible concurrency is visibility-scoped.
Inline refresh preserves scroll position and composer context when legal.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the customer-activity tab or the exact composer trigger.
routes.responsive_fallback: Customer activity remains in the same reading order; history and composer stack vertically on narrow screens.
routes.notes: Customer-visible activity is a separate stream and cannot be contaminated by internal-only facts.

source_refs.rationale: Customer-visible activity is separately stored and streamed.
The customer thread read surface and command surface are explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Screen map / 3. Page layouts / 8. Command and read API additions / 11. Accessibility and responsive rules
routes.title: Staff internal-only activity module
routes.dominant_question: What internal context, constraints, or reasoning must stay staff-only?
routes.dominant_action: Append internal notes and coordinate without contaminating customer-visible projections.
routes.artifact_posture: Internal-only notes and attachments never appear in customer-facing routes.
step_up_checkpoints: Higher-privilege note entry stays policy-gated and non-leaky.
routes.stale_view_posture: Thread stays mounted but the note composer fails closed if the workspace basis drifts.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Internal-only activity does not stale or notify customer-visible routes.
Append-only history remains visible during reconnect and rebase.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the internal-activity tab, latest note anchor, or the invoking queue row.
routes.responsive_fallback: Thread and note composer stack beneath summary content without changing visibility semantics.
routes.notes: This module is the hard internal/customer boundary inside collaboration.

source_refs.rationale: Internal-only activity must never appear in customer-visible surfaces.
The customer-visible omission and stale behavior are explicit regression scenarios.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Screen map / 3. Page layouts / 8. Command and read API additions / 11. Accessibility and responsive rules
routes.title: Staff files module
routes.dominant_question: Which artifact is current, historical, quarantined, or not safely downloadable?
routes.dominant_action: Inspect the current artifact posture before previewing, downloading, or requesting replacement.
routes.artifact_posture: Current-vs-history posture is explicit and quarantined files never masquerade as downloadable current artifacts.
step_up_checkpoints: Sensitive download or export posture remains policy-governed and may require step-up.
routes.stale_view_posture: Artifact rows stay visible as stale context but cannot be promoted to current on a drifted view.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Current and historical artifact anchors survive inline refresh.
Quarantined or replacement-required files remain visible as typed recovery context.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the exact artifact row or preview launcher that opened detail.
routes.responsive_fallback: Artifact rows stay single-column with current-state chips preserved on mobile widths.
routes.notes: The files module is the collaboration truth surface for current-vs-history artifact posture.

source_refs.rationale: The files module is a first-class workspace component.
Current-vs-history and quarantined download posture are explicit scenarios.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Screen map / 3. Page layouts / 8. Command and read API additions / 11. Accessibility and responsive rules
routes.title: Manifest route with workflow focus jump
routes.dominant_question: How does this workflow item affect the current manifest decision?
routes.dominant_action: Inspect the linked work item as subordinate context without changing the manifest shell identity.
read_surfaces: Manifest experience snapshot and stream surfaces from the northbound contract
routes.artifact_posture: Manifest truth remains primary while workflow context opens as linked support detail.
step_up_checkpoints: Manifest-local filing or approval step-up still stays route-local to the manifest shell.
routes.stale_view_posture: The manifest route remains mounted; linked workflow focus rebases inline or drops back to the manifest anchor.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Same object, same shell applies to the manifest route even when workflow focus is active.
The focus query must restore the linked work-item anchor after refresh if still legal.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the manifest-local workflow focus anchor or the parent manifest navigator.
routes.responsive_fallback: The linked workflow support view docks below the main manifest stack on narrow screens.
routes.notes: This route normalizes the explicit workflow focus deep link into the cross-surface atlas.

source_refs.rationale: The explicit manifest focus pattern is named here.
Focus jumps cannot invent a new shell identity.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Screen map / Customer request routes / 11. Accessibility and responsive rules
routes.title: Customer request list
routes.dominant_question: Which request needs a reply, upload, approval, or simply waiting?
routes.dominant_action: Open the exact request without losing customer-safe context or the return target.
read_surfaces: Customer-visible request-list projection under the collaboration contract
routes.artifact_posture: Only current safe request state and customer-safe history summaries may appear.
routes.stale_view_posture: The list refreshes in place and never shows internal-only fields even during reconnect.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Opening and closing request detail preserves the parent request-row anchor.
Cross-device continuity restores the same request when still legal.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the originating request row or list filter.
routes.responsive_fallback: Single-column list with contextual support hidden behind explicit disclosure on narrow screens.
routes.notes: The customer request list is collaboration truth rendered through portal-safe language and visibility rules.

source_refs.rationale: The route is explicit.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Shell continuity, support budget, and constrained layouts
source_refs.rationale: Client-shell continuity and return-target rules apply.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Screen map / Customer request routes / 11. Accessibility and responsive rules
routes.title: Customer request workspace
routes.dominant_question: What exactly is being asked of the client, and what is the safe next step?
routes.dominant_action: Reply, upload, or acknowledge from the same request lane without surfacing staff-only state.
routes.artifact_posture: Current artifact, history, and support handoff stay explicit and customer-safe.
step_up_checkpoints: Identity or authority verification stays inside the route rather than redirecting to a new shell.
routes.stale_view_posture: The request card stays mounted with inline recovery and explicit no-safe-action language when needed.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Return from browser or authority handoff never implies completion until the request projection settles.
Historical artifacts remain visible as context but never become the default current artifact on rebase.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the request focus anchor, current artifact row, or the invoking request list row.
routes.responsive_fallback: Primary task column fills the viewport; history and help collapse behind explicit disclosure on mobile widths.
routes.notes: This is the customer-safe collaboration detail route that shares portal shell law without inheriting staff-only vocabulary.

source_refs.rationale: The route is explicit.
Customer-safe return, handoff, and artifact posture rules are shared.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: Navigation contract / Route architecture / Accessibility and interaction rules / Read-model and API translation requirements
routes.title: Portal home
routes.dominant_question: What changed, what matters now, and what should the client do next?
routes.dominant_action: Choose the next routed task from one status hero and one task queue.
routes.artifact_posture: Home uses summary-first current-state cards; artifacts stay subordinate to task selection.
step_up_checkpoints: Home may route into in-place step-up, but does not force a new top-level destination.
routes.stale_view_posture: The hero and task queue remain visible while stale or reconnect posture is explained inline.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Home must restore the pending task, upload, approval pack, or onboarding draft across devices.
Contextual routes are not allowed to become a sixth permanent tab.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the status hero primary action or the exact task queue row that launched a deeper route.
routes.responsive_fallback: Home stays single-column and mobile-first; support remains subordinate to the task queue.
routes.notes: The atlas binds Home to `/portal` as the canonical root route because the contract names Home but not `/portal/home`.

source_refs.rationale: Home is one of the five permanent destinations.
Home layout modules are explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: Navigation contract / Route architecture / Accessibility and interaction rules / Read-model and API translation requirements
routes.title: Portal documents
routes.dominant_question: Which document request is active, and what is the exact upload posture?
routes.dominant_action: Upload, retry, or replace inside the same request lane with confidence and binding made explicit.
routes.artifact_posture: Transfer, scan, validation, accepted, rejected, superseded, and replacement-only states stay visibly distinct.
step_up_checkpoints: Sensitive uploads remain request-bound and may require route-local confirmation before final attachment.
routes.stale_view_posture: The request card stays mounted with request-version posture explicit during rebase or reconnect.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Resumed uploads preserve frozen tenant, client, request, and request-version identity.
Rejected and superseded uploads remain visible as recovery context but never become default current artifacts.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the request card, current upload row, or upload affordance that initiated the transfer.
routes.responsive_fallback: On mobile the active request card owns the full viewport and history/help collapse behind disclosure.
routes.notes: Upload action families are normalized from prose because the contract names the flow and the byte-session exception, not a complete command enum.

source_refs.rationale: The upload phases and request-binding rules are explicit.
Upload session allocation is the sole non-command mutation surface for raw bytes.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: Navigation contract / Route architecture / Accessibility and interaction rules / Read-model and API translation requirements
routes.title: Portal approvals
routes.dominant_question: What is being approved, and is sign-off safe right now?
routes.dominant_action: Review change digest, declaration, and sign-off inside one contained route.
routes.artifact_posture: Current approval pack and signed receipt stay explicit; superseded packs remain historical only.
step_up_checkpoints: Step-up stays contained inside the route and must not route-switch the client into a different shell.
routes.stale_view_posture: The approval pack remains visible while stale-signoff posture blocks unsafe commitment.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Return from external sign-off handoff never implies completion until the governed approval read model settles.
Historical approval packs remain available but cannot become the current default by drift.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the digest anchor, declaration section, or the sign-off trigger.
routes.responsive_fallback: The active approval pack takes full width on mobile and keeps sticky sign-off controls visible without covering governing explanation.
routes.notes: Approval command names are normalized from route-local action prose rather than a published command enum.

source_refs.rationale: Approval posture, step-up, and stale protection are explicit.
Rebased approval and historical pack posture are explicit scenarios.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: Navigation contract / Route architecture / Accessibility and interaction rules / Read-model and API translation requirements
routes.title: Portal onboarding
routes.dominant_question: Which onboarding step is active, what is saved, and what still needs completion?
routes.dominant_action: Advance the current onboarding step without losing entered answers, uploads, or support context.
routes.artifact_posture: Onboarding confirmations and route-local uploads stay subordinate to the active step workspace.
step_up_checkpoints: Identity and eligibility checks stay inside the journey step where they are relevant.
routes.stale_view_posture: Previously entered answers and resumable draft posture stay visible during refresh, reconnect, and rebase.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Save-and-return preserves the current step, answers, and any in-progress upload sessions.
Onboarding is only a permanent destination while active.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the active step header, current form field group, or support entry anchor.
routes.responsive_fallback: The active onboarding step fills the viewport on mobile while support collapses behind explicit entry.
routes.notes: Onboarding continuity is route-local and must not be reinterpreted as a general portal dashboard state.

source_refs.rationale: Step save-and-return and support posture are explicit.
Mobile-width onboarding rules are explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: Navigation contract / Route architecture / Accessibility and interaction rules / Read-model and API translation requirements
routes.title: Portal help
routes.dominant_question: What help path matches the current problem without losing route context?
routes.dominant_action: Open or resume support using context-preserving case materialization.
routes.artifact_posture: Support history and current case context stay explicit and subordinate to the active help action.
step_up_checkpoints: Escalated help flows may invoke route-local verification while preserving the case context panel.
routes.stale_view_posture: The case context panel stays mounted and makes hidden/unavailable states plain-language rather than implicit.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Help requests preserve route, reason family, and context bundle when resumed.
Return targets after support handoff restore the originating route and focus anchor.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the support entry trigger, top question row, or the originating route action that opened Help.
routes.responsive_fallback: Help stays single-column with context tucked beneath the primary support options on mobile widths.
routes.notes: Help is the only portal route where the support surface is allowed to foreground itself.

source_refs.rationale: Help route modules are explicit.
The durable `CLIENT_PORTAL_REQUEST_HELP` command is explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Profile boundary and shell contract / 4. Information architecture and route map / 6. Shared interaction and mutation rules / 8. Accessibility and responsive requirements
routes.title: Governance overview
routes.dominant_question: Where is governance attention required right now?
routes.dominant_action: Open the highest-priority risk, queue item, or route slice without losing the current governance basis.
routes.artifact_posture: Overview is summary-first and never exports broader detail than the current filtered slice permits.
step_up_checkpoints: Risky follow-on routes may trigger inline step-up before mutation staging begins.
routes.stale_view_posture: Overview cards stay mounted while stale basis or access drift is surfaced inline.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Selected object, dominant question, and filters survive responsive collapse and browser back.
Overview attention rows preserve the same route anchor across refresh.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the overview attention summary row or pending change queue item that launched deeper work.
routes.responsive_fallback: On narrower widths the inventory rail compresses to a tray and the audit sidecar redocks beneath the canvas.
routes.notes: Overview keeps the five-region governance grammar visible even when density collapses responsively.

source_refs.rationale: Overview widgets and route purpose are explicit.
The overview endpoint is explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Profile boundary and shell contract / 4. Information architecture and route map / 6. Shared interaction and mutation rules / 8. Accessibility and responsive requirements
routes.title: Tenant configuration workspace
routes.dominant_question: What tenant-wide change is proposed, and what is its blast radius?
routes.dominant_action: Stage diff-first changes into the basket and seek approval without hiding impact.
routes.artifact_posture: Diffs, blast radius, and history are first-class and remain visible beside staged change posture.
step_up_checkpoints: Approval submission and any privilege-widening change remain inline and basis-hash guarded.
routes.stale_view_posture: The staged diff remains visible but cannot commit once policy snapshot or dependency topology drifts.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Change basket retains context across refresh, reconnect, and responsive collapse.
The exact basis hash must be preserved when approving or submitting staged changes.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the staged diff row, change basket item, or approval composer trigger.
routes.responsive_fallback: Sidecar redocks under the workspace and the basket remains visible before any history panel on narrow screens.
routes.notes: Mutation families are normalized from the governance route prose; the northbound contract instead pins stale guards and basis hashes.

source_refs.rationale: Tenant config widgets and basket/approval posture are explicit.
Policy snapshot and dependency topology hashes are explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Profile boundary and shell contract / 4. Information architecture and route map / 6. Shared interaction and mutation rules / 8. Accessibility and responsive requirements
routes.title: Principal access directory
routes.dominant_question: Who currently has access, and how was it granted?
routes.dominant_action: Inspect principal access posture and stage adjustments without losing chain-of-authority context.
routes.artifact_posture: Principal detail, authority chain, and staged access changes remain side-by-side.
step_up_checkpoints: Any privilege-widening change must preserve simulation basis and step-up posture.
routes.stale_view_posture: Principal detail stays visible while mutation controls fail closed when access basis drifts.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Roving selection preserves the selected principal under responsive collapse.
Authority chain detail remains attached to the selected principal rather than resetting to list top.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the principal directory row or authority-chain invocation anchor.
routes.responsive_fallback: Directory compresses to a tray but selected principal context persists into the canvas.
routes.notes: This route preserves the governance five-layer access explanation stack in detail form.

source_refs.rationale: Principal route purpose and detail are explicit.
The principals endpoint is explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Profile boundary and shell contract / 4. Information architecture and route map / 6. Shared interaction and mutation rules / 8. Accessibility and responsive requirements
routes.title: Role template matrix
routes.dominant_question: What does each role grant, and how would a change alter effective authority?
routes.dominant_action: Stage role definition changes against the matrix with diff-first review.
routes.artifact_posture: Role diffs, impact, and history remain visible before commit.
step_up_checkpoints: Role changes that widen authority preserve both topology and simulation basis hashes.
routes.stale_view_posture: Matrix stays visible while mutation affordances fail closed on outdated basis.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Selected role and visible diff row survive refresh and browser back.
Role edits preserve the current comparison anchor after re-render.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the selected role row, matrix cell, or change basket invocation.
routes.responsive_fallback: Matrix columns compress before the sidecar; detailed diff remains explicit.
routes.notes: The roles route is dense but never allowed to hide diff and blast-radius posture behind collapsible noise.

source_refs.rationale: Role route purpose follows the access map.
The role matrix endpoint is explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Profile boundary and shell contract / 4. Information architecture and route map / 6. Shared interaction and mutation rules / 8. Accessibility and responsive requirements
routes.title: Access simulator
routes.dominant_question: What would authorization do under the exact current basis and topology?
routes.dominant_action: Run a simulation and, when lawful, stage a mutation against that exact basis.
routes.artifact_posture: Simulation basis, dependency topology, and potential blast radius are treated as first-class artifacts.
step_up_checkpoints: Committing a simulated change requires the exact simulation basis hash and any route-local step-up.
routes.stale_view_posture: Simulation result remains visible but cannot be committed after basis or topology drift.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Simulation basis hash and dependency topology hash are preserved across receipts and retries.
The simulator cannot silently rerun under a different basis when resumed.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the simulation parameter set, result card, or commit trigger.
routes.responsive_fallback: Controls collapse above results on narrow widths while preserving the same simulation anchor.
routes.notes: The simulator route is the most explicit place where governance basis hashes become user-visible product law.

source_refs.rationale: The simulator route is explicit.
The simulation endpoint and basis-hash rules are explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Profile boundary and shell contract / 4. Information architecture and route map / 6. Shared interaction and mutation rules / 8. Accessibility and responsive requirements
routes.title: Authority link inventory
routes.dominant_question: Which authority links are healthy, mismatched, drifting, or missing delegation?
routes.dominant_action: Inspect health and stage repairs without collapsing mismatch categories together.
routes.artifact_posture: Binding mismatch, delegation gap, and environment drift are distinct and remain inspectable as separate artifacts.
step_up_checkpoints: Repair commands stay basis-hash guarded and may demand step-up for authority-widening effects.
routes.stale_view_posture: Health detail remains visible while repair actions fail closed on outdated inventory or topology basis.
recovery_and_resume_rules: The selected link item remains anchored through refresh and export eligibility checks.
Repair posture cannot silently downgrade mismatch severity or merge categories after resume.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the selected authority-link row or detail expansion trigger.
routes.responsive_fallback: Inventory rows collapse into a tray while detail and health timeline stay accessible in the canvas.
routes.notes: Authority link health categories stay deliberately distinct to prevent semantic flattening in later UI work.

source_refs.rationale: The route semantics are explicit.
The authority-links endpoint is explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Profile boundary and shell contract / 4. Information architecture and route map / 6. Shared interaction and mutation rules / 8. Accessibility and responsive requirements
routes.title: Retention policy matrix
routes.dominant_question: Which retention minimums and exceptions govern this tenant right now?
routes.dominant_action: Review or stage policy changes with explicit impact preview and legal hold interaction.
routes.artifact_posture: Policy matrix and impact preview remain paired; export follows the active filtered slice only.
step_up_checkpoints: Lowering retention minimums or widening erase posture remains step-up and approval guarded.
routes.stale_view_posture: The matrix remains visible while commit actions fail closed on drifted retention frames.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Retention focus order is normalized from the shared governance shell law plus route-local workspaces.
Selected policy row and impact preview remain attached under responsive collapse.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the selected policy row or impact preview toggle.
routes.responsive_fallback: Policy matrix compacts before impact preview disappears; sidecar redocks beneath the canvas.
routes.notes: This route uses a normalized landmark order because the contract names the workspace more strongly than exact landmark sequencing.

source_refs.rationale: Retention routes are explicit.
The retention endpoint is explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Profile boundary and shell contract / 4. Information architecture and route map / 6. Shared interaction and mutation rules / 8. Accessibility and responsive requirements
routes.title: Legal hold register
routes.dominant_question: Which legal holds are active, and what do they block or override?
routes.dominant_action: Open or release legal holds with explicit downstream impact in view.
routes.artifact_posture: Hold records, policy impacts, and downstream constraints remain visible together.
step_up_checkpoints: Opening or releasing a hold requires inline step-up and approval posture when policy demands it.
routes.stale_view_posture: Register entries remain visible but irreversible actions fail closed on stale retention basis.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Legal hold selection and impact preview remain anchored under layout collapse.
Hold state is never inferred from a policy matrix alone; the register stays authoritative.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the legal-hold row or impact preview trigger.
routes.responsive_fallback: Register stays primary; impact preview and audit sidecar redock beneath the main workspace on narrow widths.
routes.notes: Legal holds remain explicitly distinct from broader retention policy edits.

source_refs.rationale: The route is explicit.
The retention frame is the northbound source for legal-hold slices.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Profile boundary and shell contract / 4. Information architecture and route map / 6. Shared interaction and mutation rules / 8. Accessibility and responsive requirements
routes.title: Erasure queue
routes.dominant_question: Which erasure requests are eligible, blocked, or irreversible?
routes.dominant_action: Inspect and approve erasure only when the current retention frame and legal-hold state permit it.
routes.artifact_posture: Eligibility, blocking holds, and irreversible consequences are all first-class rows, not hidden edge cases.
step_up_checkpoints: Irreversible erasure actions are never accepted from a stale governance view.
routes.stale_view_posture: Queue context remains visible while erase actions fail closed with typed stale-view errors.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Selected erasure row and blocker detail stay mounted across refresh and back.
The route preserves the same filtered slice for export and receipt review.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the erasure queue row or blocker explanation anchor.
routes.responsive_fallback: Queue remains first; blocker detail redocks beneath it before any loss of semantic meaning.
routes.notes: This route is the clearest example of the governance stale-view hard stop on irreversible action.

source_refs.rationale: The route is explicit.
Unmasked audit export and irreversible retention actions cannot commit from stale views.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 2. Profile boundary and shell contract / 4. Information architecture and route map / 6. Shared interaction and mutation rules / 8. Accessibility and responsive requirements
routes.title: Audit investigation workbench
routes.dominant_question: What happened, when, why, and what evidence is export-eligible now?
routes.dominant_action: Investigate append-only event slices and compare diffs without altering the audit trail.
routes.artifact_posture: Append-only event slices, correlation neighborhoods, and export eligibility remain explicit and non-destructive.
step_up_checkpoints: Export remains governed by the active filtered slice and masking/export posture.
routes.stale_view_posture: Investigation frames stay visible while export or follow-on actions fail closed when masking or basis drift.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Selected event, diff inspector state, and export eligibility panel survive refresh and responsive collapse.
Audit slices remain append-only and never reorder into heuristic summaries.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the selected event row or export eligibility trigger.
routes.responsive_fallback: The audit sidecar redocks but remains visible before export posture is obscured.
routes.notes: This route is read-heavy and append-only, but still governed by the same return-target and support-region laws.

source_refs.rationale: Audit route purpose is explicit.
The audit-investigations endpoint is explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 4. Platform translation map / 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / 6. Data flow and synchronization model / 8. Persistence model
routes.title: Primary manifest scene
routes.dominant_question: What is the current manifest decision state, and what support detail belongs in the inspector or a detached window?
routes.dominant_action: Operate on the same manifest object through sidebar, canvas, and inspector without violating browser truth.
visibility_lanes: CALM operator truth after northbound reconciliation
routes.artifact_posture: Evidence preview, compare context, and pinned selections stay inside cache-safe native affordances.
step_up_checkpoints: Native step-up still delegates to governed backend/browser flows rather than inventing offline legal commands.
routes.stale_view_posture: Previously valid content stays mounted while inspector actions fail closed and refresh/rebase becomes explicit.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Scene restoration never reopens a manifest after tenant switch, privilege downgrade, or masking drift.
Resize or inspector collapse must preserve the same manifest scene identity, dominant question, and settlement posture.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the manifest sidebar row, canvas anchor, or inspector trigger that opened support detail.
routes.responsive_fallback: Sidebar and inspector collapse independently but the same object remains mounted in the center canvas.
routes.notes: Native primary scenes embody calm-shell law; they do not define a fourth shell family.

source_refs.rationale: The primary split-view scene is explicit.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: Native secondary windows and preview subjects
source_refs.rationale: Scene legality is cache-bound.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 4. Platform translation map / 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / 6. Data flow and synchronization model / 8. Persistence model
routes.title: Primary work-item scene
routes.dominant_question: What is the safest next action on this work item from the native scene?
routes.dominant_action: Work the same collaboration object through native navigation, inspector, and shortcuts while honoring web truth.
visibility_lanes: CALM operator truth after northbound reconciliation
routes.artifact_posture: Current workspace detail may be cached locally, but mutations still respect stale-view guards from the rendered surface.
step_up_checkpoints: No blind offline legal commands; backend stale-view guards still bind every mutation-capable affordance.
routes.stale_view_posture: The scene stays mounted but any stale command lane downgrades inline to read-only or refresh-required posture.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Reconnect and rebase must match the browser collaboration contract.
Scene restoration keeps the same work item, dominant question, and settlement posture when still legal.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the invoking sidebar item, selected module anchor, or inspector trigger.
routes.responsive_fallback: Scene collapses from three-pane to two-pane without replacing the work-item object or hiding the inspector law.
routes.notes: The work-item scene is the native mirror of the collaboration workspace, with native command affordances layered on top.

source_refs.rationale: Work-item data flow and cursor/resume model are explicit.
Rebase, stale-view, and scene-identity parity are explicit acceptance criteria.

routes.title: Detached compare window
routes.object_ownership: Manifest lineage or drift comparison subject
routes.dominant_question: How do two evidence or decision states compare without collapsing the parent scene context?
routes.dominant_action: Inspect compare detail in a support-only window and return to the parent anchor.
read_surfaces: Parent scene export to detached compare window
routes.artifact_posture: Compare windows are support-only and cannot silently widen preview scope beyond the parent subject.
routes.stale_view_posture: If the parent scene basis drifts, the compare window becomes read-only or closes back to the parent anchor.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Detached windows serialize as `NativeOperatorSecondaryWindowScene` with parent return metadata.
Preview subject legality is rechecked before scene restoration.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the exact parent compare trigger or selected evidence row.
routes.responsive_fallback: Secondary windows are native overlays; they do not collapse into browser route variants.
routes.notes: Secondary windows are deliberately support-only overlays, not new shell families.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / 8. Persistence model / 11. Security and runtime posture
routes.title: Detached audit window
routes.dominant_question: What audit or provenance detail supports the current scene without displacing it?
routes.dominant_action: Inspect append-only audit detail in a parent-bound support window.
routes.artifact_posture: Audit detail stays non-authoritative locally and must respect masking/export posture at open time and restore time.
step_up_checkpoints: Export or print remains governed by masking/export posture before any helper opens.
routes.stale_view_posture: Audit content may remain visible but any export or print action fails closed on drift.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Parent focus restoration is serialized and mandatory.
Quick Look, print, and export helpers purge temp artifacts if tenant, masking, or preview subject drift.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the parent audit trigger or provenance row anchor.
routes.responsive_fallback: Window remains detached rather than collapsing into a route-local drawer.
routes.notes: Audit windows inherit native support-only rules and masking-aware restore envelopes.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / Secondary windows
source_refs.rationale: Audit windows are explicit.
Quick Look and export legality are explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / 8. Persistence model / 11. Security and runtime posture
routes.title: Detached filing-packet window
routes.dominant_question: What packet material is ready for review, export, or print under current masking posture?
routes.dominant_action: Review or export packet detail in a support-only window tied to the invoking parent scene.
routes.artifact_posture: Print-preview and export staging are governed helpers, not independent truth surfaces.
step_up_checkpoints: Export and print helpers must recheck masking/export posture before opening or restoring.
routes.stale_view_posture: Packet preview may persist visually while export controls fail closed on drift.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Temp artifacts purge when tenant, masking, or preview subject drift.
The secondary window must restore back to the exact parent packet trigger.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the parent packet review trigger or selected evidence row.
routes.responsive_fallback: Detached packet review stays a windowed support surface rather than a route mutation.
routes.notes: Packet review is explicitly support-only and inherits the parent scene legality envelope.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / Secondary windows
source_refs.rationale: Filing packet review/export windows are explicit.
Temporary artifact purge rules are explicit.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / 8. Persistence model / 11. Security and runtime posture
routes.title: Detached authority review window
routes.dominant_question: What authority interaction detail belongs in support context without widening the parent scene?
routes.dominant_action: Inspect authority detail and return to the parent work surface.
routes.artifact_posture: Authority review stays tied to the invoking object and cannot survive into a broader context.
step_up_checkpoints: Any authority action still routes through governed backend or browser step-up paths.
routes.stale_view_posture: Authority review can stay visible, but action affordances fail closed when the parent basis drifts.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Detached authority review restores to the exact parent invocation anchor.
Route legality is rechecked before the detached window resumes.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the parent authority review trigger.
routes.responsive_fallback: Detached authority review is never collapsed into a new browser route or shell family.
routes.notes: Authority review is support-only and parent-bound like the other detached native windows.

source_refs.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / Secondary windows
source_refs.rationale: Authority interaction review is explicit.
Authority work still uses governed browser/session flows when needed.

routes.source_heading_or_logical_block: 5. Preferred window and scene architecture / 8. Persistence model / 11. Security and runtime posture
routes.title: Native browser auth handoff
routes.object_ownership: Temporary external identity or authority handoff
routes.dominant_question: What external authority or identity task must complete before the parent scene can settle?
routes.dominant_action: Leave the native scene temporarily, complete the external authority step, and resume the parent scene with the same return target.
routes.artifact_posture: No external handoff implies completion until the parent governed read model confirms settlement.
step_up_checkpoints: The handoff itself is the step-up checkpoint and must serialize the parent return target.
routes.stale_view_posture: Parent scene remains preserved behind the handoff and resumes only after legality checks and fresh read settlement.
recovery_and_resume_rules: Temporary external handoff is allowed but must restore the exact parent route/scene and focus anchor.
Tenant switch, privilege downgrade, or masking drift invalidate the return envelope rather than resuming heuristically.
routes.focus_return_rule: Return to the exact parent scene trigger that initiated the handoff.
routes.responsive_fallback: The handoff is external, not responsive; the parent scene remains preserved for resume or explicit invalidation.
routes.notes: External handoff is a temporary escape hatch, not a shell transition.

source_refs.rationale: ASWebAuthenticationSession is explicit.
External handoff must return to the same object and shell.
